title: wholesome canadian almond butter roast
description: A light, warm, and comforting post-workout dish featuring almond butter, simmered with Canadian flavors.
instructions: Peel and chop the sweet potatoes into 1-inch cubes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the almond butter, maple syrup, coconut oil, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt until smooth.
Add the chopped sweet potatoes to the bowl and toss until they're evenly coated with the almond butter mixture.
Transfer the sweet potatoes to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and roast for 20 minutes.
Remove the sweet potatoes from the oven and sprinkle with fresh parsley and thyme.
Simmer for 5 minutes before serving, if desired for an extra boost of flavor.
Garnish with additional parsley and thyme, if desired.
Store leftovers in the fridge for up to 3 days.
Reheat in the oven or microwave before serving.
Be gentle when reheating, as the almond butter can separate and become grainy.
If you don't have sweet potatoes, you can substitute with other root vegetables like carrots or parsnips.
You can also add other spices or herbs to the almond butter mixture to suit your taste.
This dish is vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free, making it a great option for those with dietary restrictions.
